rationale: Hit the user-requested 20-video baseline by adding 4 NEW videos beyond the existing 18. Variety: 1 viral hit (~21k views), 1 high-performer (~4k), 1 mid (~2.9k), 1 long-tail mid (~1.9k). Topical mix: LLM tokenization, LLM memory/quantization, ML fundamentals (backprop), OS internals (Linux fork). All Shorts (45-60s vertical).

newly_picked.title: Why ChatGPT can't count the R's in strawberry
newly_picked.reason: Viral hit (21k views — 4x his average). Classic tokenizer-explainer topic; likely strong character/letter visual primitives.

newly_picked.title: Google just shrunk LLM memory 5x — here's how TurboQuant works
newly_picked.reason: High performer in his top tier. Memory + compression topic likely to surface NEW bar/gauge primitives (memory reduction visualizations).

newly_picked.title: How neural networks actually learn — backpropagation in 45 seconds, from miss to math
newly_picked.reason: ML fundamentals — backprop is graph/arrow heavy. Expected: gradient flow animations, weight update sequences. Tests catalog coverage of math/arrow primitives.

newly_picked.title: Linux fork() clones a 4GB process in under a millisecond by copying nothing
newly_picked.reason: Systems/OS topic (vs the heavily LLM-focused existing 18). Copy-on-write is a well-known visualization opportunity: parent/child process trees, shared memory pages. Tests if Adam's templates generalize beyond AI topics.
